element_introduction_order.element: Basic run-and-gun platforming with standard weaponry
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Establishes core movement and shooting mechanics before introducing variants
Players discover new weapons within levels, expanding tactical options
Adds verticality and environmental traversal puzzle-solving
Temporary power enhancement for specific challenges
Major gameplay shift requiring adaptation to new movement/perception rules

element_introduction_order.element: Environmental hazards (exploding sheep, embedded floor/wall hazards)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Requires pattern recognition and precise timing
Genre-breaking puzzle format with absurdist answers; low mechanical difficulty but high absurdity
Inverted gravity control; avoidance-focused rather than combat-focused

element_introduction_order.element: Boss evasion (Evil the Cat attacks during float phase)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Adds pressure to an already difficult vertical-avoidance sequence
Complete perspective shift; requires spatial reorientation and defensive positioning
Added pressure beyond personal survival; must defend vulnerable objective

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Level 9: See Jim Run Run Jim Run (final level)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Culminating challenge testing all learned skills under time pressure against an AI opponent

typical_relief_triggers: Safe traversal sections between combat arenas
Absurdist humor breaks (trivia segments, character transformations)
Power-up discovery and temporary invulnerability states
Genre shifts that provide mental/mechanical novelty rather than pure difficulty
typical_tension_triggers: Boss appearances (Evil the Cat, Major Mucus, Psy-Crow)
Objective pressure (protecting escort item, racing against opponent)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Front-loaded difficulty with moderate early game, ramping significantly mid-game through genre-breaking level variety, culminating in final race. Relief comes through tonal absurdism and mechanical novelty rather than difficulty reduction.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate. Standard platformer mechanics with expanded moveset (auto-aiming guns, Snott mechanics) introduced gradually.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Steep increase. Levels 3-6 introduce radical mechanic shifts (cave salamander disguise with trivia, floating head evasion, isometric perspective) that require complete playstyle adaptation.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained high difficulty. Final level combines learned mechanics in a race format, testing reflexes and pattern recognition simultaneously.
notable_spikes: Level 3 (Villi People): Shift from action to trivia, hazard gauntlet
Level 5 (Inflated Head): Inverted gravity + boss pressure, no solid attack options
Level 6 (The Flyin' King): Complete perspective and input model change with escort objective
Level 9 (See Jim Run): Final gauntlet combining sprint mechanics with obstacle avoidance under AI pressure

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels that abandon run-and-gun formula entirely for thematic variants (trivia game show, floatation puzzle, isometric shooter). Creates tonal whiplash and resets player skill expectations.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 3 (Villi People), Level 5 (Inflated Head), Level 6 (The Flyin' King)

notable_level_patterns.description: Traditional side-scrolling platformer with combat focus, expanded weapon variety, and environmental platforming.
Instead of stationary boss battle, final encounter is a chase sequence through an obstacle course against Psy-Crow, combining all learned mechanics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 9 (See Jim Run Run Jim Run)
